input.prompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

input.initialPrompt: A .NET solution build has failed. The binary log from the failed build is available at build.binlog in the current directory. You do NOT have access to the original source files — only the binlog. Analyze this binlog file to diagnose all build failures, identify which errors are root causes versus cascading failures from dependent project failures, and suggest targeted fixes for each root cause.

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command: which dotnet && dotnet --version 2>/dev/null; which msbuild 2>/dev/null; ls -lh build.binlog
arguments.description: Check available .NET tools and binlog file

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Check available .NET tools and binlog file

data.reasoningText:
The user wants me to analyze a .NET build failure using a binary log file (build.binlog). Let me start by examining the binlog file to understand what went wrong.

I'll need to use `dotnet` tools to analyze the binlog. The standard approach is to use `dotnet build` with the binlog viewer or use the MSBuild structured log viewer. Let me check what tools are available and then analyze the binlog.

Let me first check if dotnet is available, and then use it to analyze the binlog.

arguments.description: Check available .NET tools and binlog file

toolRequests.name: bash

arguments.command:
# Install the structured log viewer CLI tool to parse binlogs
dotnet tool install --global dotnet-structuredlogviewer 2>/dev/null || true
# Also try the MSBuild log replay approach - replay the binlog to extract errors/warnings
dotnet build build.binlog --verbosity:quiet 2>&1 | head -200
arguments.description: Try replaying binlog to extract errors

toolRequests.intentionSummary: Try replaying binlog to extract errors
